title: Glandularia andina
intro: Glandularia andina, also known as Andean verbena or Chilean verbena, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Verbenaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia andina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Glandularia andina is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to treat various health conditions and promote well-being.
taxonomy: Glandularia andina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of South America and belongs to the genus Glandularia. This species is closely related to other members of the genus, such as Glandularia pucana and Glandularia cordifolia. Glandularia andina is a perennial plant that produces small, white or pink flowers. It is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the Verbenaceae family.
